eason_chin.name: Eason Chin
eason_chin.summary:
A California competitor, Eason Chin has collected 3 tournament titles, currently competing at the U13 level, capturing 3 titles across 10 tournaments and 23 entries. Each win is earned through bracket battles against hungry opponents — and Eason keeps finding ways to come out on top.

A true triple-threat, Eason competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 2 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (4 entries).

Eason has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 5 different players across the career:
  • Yuki Guan (current primary partner) — 4 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (4 total events in XD, across 2 seasons)
  • Joey Wang (current partner) — 1 events, 1 title together in BD
  • Allen Wu — 6 events, 1 title together in BD

Eason is peaking at the right time in 2023-2024, with 2 titles across 2 tournaments — a clear sign that Eason is still ascending.

Eason has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NorCal, SoCal, Eason isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Eason brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

Hailing from Illinois, Erica Chen Lin has climbed to the top of the mountain at Junior Nationals, the crown jewel of the American junior badminton season in XD U15. That title represents the culmination of grueling competition against the very best in the country. With 9 career victories across 22 tournaments, Erica has proven the ability to deliver when it matters most.

Notably, Erica has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Erica is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 2 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, 5 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (5 entries) • Junior Nationals champion (XD U15) • 1 ORC title.

Erica has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 17 different players across the career:
  • William Geng (current primary partner) — 4 events together this season alone with 2 titles, building serious momentum (4 total events in XD, across 2 seasons)
  • Jacob Ma (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in Mixed/XD (3 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)
  • Jade Hu (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in GD (3 events, best: #3)
  • Jayden Mar (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events)

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 5 podium finishes across 6 tournaments. Erica is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Erica has competed across U13, U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Erica has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 9 seeded entries across the career, Erica consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 9 titles, Erica is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.
